item.title: 模因星期一 | Meme Monday
item.description:
本·哈尔彭

发表于 6 月 12 日

模因星期一！

今天的封面图来自上周的话题。

低趣味的幽默会被模组否决。

一旦被暂停，Ben 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，ben 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是您可以用来标记 ben 的操作：

降低 ben 的所有帖子的可见性

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning ben 将恢复他们帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ben Halpern

Posted on Jun 12

 

Meme Monday!

Today's cover image comes from last week's thread.

DEV is an inclusive space! Humor in poor taste will be downvoted by mods.

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ben:

Make all posts by ben less visible

ben consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: ben

item.title: 对一次性代码使用单一输入 | Using a Single Input for one-time-code
item.description:
前一天晚上，我在尝试创建单输入一次性代码时获得了一些乐趣：

一次性代码是一个有效的autocomplete值，通过几行 JS，它可以让您从文本消息 (sms) 中填写字段。

不过，更常见的是，它被称为OTP 。

来自维基百科：

一次性密码 (OTP)，也称为一次性 PIN、一次性授权码 (OTAC) 或动态密码，是一种在计算机系统或其他系统上仅对一次登录会话或交易有效的密码数字设备。

回到我的例子。

它使用非常简单的标记：

 <input type="text" autocomplete="one-time-code" inputmode="numeric" maxlength="6" pattern="\d{6}" >

这是一堆模拟 6 个字段的东西（来自属性--otc-digits ），而实际上 - 它只是一个<input> 。 它必须使用等宽字体，所以1ch的神奇值起作用了——同样适用于letter-spacing 。 但为什么？

您以前创建过 OTP 组件吗？

我正在编写“组件”，因为它通常是一个<fieldset>带有六个<input>和一堆 JavaScript 来检测您何时进入或离开字段等。

当您从Web OTP API填写字段时，您需要拆分值，并填写六个字段而不是一个。

使用单个输入就简单得多：

 navigator.credentials.get({ otp: {transport:['sms']} }) .then(otp => input.value = otp.code);

突出显示当前“字段”

单输入 OTP 并不完美。 当您从“字段”移动到“字段”时，如果插入符是一个块，那将是更好的用户体验：

 .selector { caret-shape: block; }

不幸的是，还没有浏览器支持caret-shape 。

另一种方法是添加另一个background-gradient ，但不重复该模式：

并通过将大小属性 — --_otp-bgsz — 与数字编号--_otp-digit相乘来定位它作为自定义属性：

 .selector { background-position: calc(var(--_otp-digit, 0) * var(--_otp-bgsz)) 0; }

这并不完美，因为我们需要将数字放在 CSS 自定义属性中，然后使用 JavaScript 更新它：

 input.addEventListener('input', () => input.style.setProperty('--_otp-digit', input.selectionStart) )

这可以用更简单的方式完成吗？ 其他建议？ 请使用评论！

这是一个 Codepen 演示：



原文:

The other night I had a bit of fun trying to create a single-input one-time-code:

one-time-code is a valid autocomplete-value, and with a few lines of JS, it let's you fill out the field from a text-message (sms).

More often, it's called OTP, though.

From Wikipedia:

A one-time password (OTP), also known as a one-time PIN, one-time authorization code (OTAC) or dynamic password, is a password that is valid for only one login session or transaction, on a computer system or other digital device.

Back to my example.

It's using very simple markup:

<input type="text" autocomplete="one-time-code" inputmode="numeric" maxlength="6" pattern="\d{6}"
>

The CSS is a bit more complex:

:where([autocomplete=one-time-code]) { --otp-digits: 6; /* length */ --otc-ls: 2ch; --otc-gap: 1.25; /* private consts */ --_otp-bgsz: calc(var(--otc-ls) + 1ch); all: unset; background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--otc-bg, #EEE) calc(var(--otc-gap) * var(--otc-ls)), transparent 0 ) 0 0 / var(--_otp-bgsz) 100%; caret-color: var(--otc-cc, #333); clip-path: inset(0% calc(var(--otc-ls) / 2) 0% 0%); font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: var(--otc-fz, 2.5em); inline-size: calc(var(--otc-digits) * var(--_otp-bgsz)); letter-spacing: var(--otc-ls); padding-block: var(--otc-pb, 1ch); padding-inline-start: calc(((var(--otc-ls) - 1ch) / 2) * var(--otc-gap));
}

It's a bunch of stuff simulating 6 fields (from the property --otc-digits), while — in reality — it's just a single <input>. The spacing between the "fields" is due to letter-spacing, and the gray "boxes" are from a linear-gradient.

It has to use a monospace-font, so the magic value of 1ch works — same applies to the letter-spacing. 1ch equals the width of a zero.

But why?

Have you ever created a OTP-component before?

I'm writing "component", because it's typically a <fieldset> with six <input>s and a bunch of JavaScript to detect when you enter- or leave a field etc.

When you fill out the field from the Web OTP API, you need to split the value, and fill-in six fields instead of one.

With a single input it's much simpler:

navigator.credentials.get({ otp: {transport:['sms']}
})
.then(otp => input.value = otp.code);

Highlightning the current "field"

The single-input OTP is not perfect. When you move from "field" to "field", it would be a better user-experience, if the caret was a block:

.selector { caret-shape: block;
}

Unfortunately, no browsers support caret-shape yet.

Another way, would be to add another background-gradient, but without repeating the pattern:

And position it by multiplying the size-property — --_otp-bgsz — with the digit-number, --_otp-digit, as a custom property:

.selector { background-position: calc(var(--_otp-digit, 0) * var(--_otp-bgsz)) 0;
}

This is not perfect, because we'd need to put the digit in a CSS Custom Property, and then update that with JavaScript:

input.addEventListener('input', () => input.style.setProperty('--_otp-digit', input.selectionStart)
)

Could this be done in an even simpler way? Other sugestions? Please use the comments!

Here's a Codepen-demo:
item.author: madsstoumann

item.title: 在 Typescript 中创建和发布 GitHub 操作 - ULTIMATE 2023 指南 | Create and Publish a GitHub Action in Typescript - ULTIMATE 2023 GUIDE
item.description:
它们可用于运行测试、部署代码、发布包等等。

很棒的是，有一个 GitHub Actions Marketplace，您可以在其中找到由...社区创建的许多操作。 但是，如果找不到所需的操作怎么办？ 您可以创建自己的并在那里发布！

在本教程中，我们将详细了解如何：

  • 在 Typescript 中创建 GitHub 操作
  • 扩展我们的 Action 以支持自定义输入
  • 与 GitHub 的 API 集成，为 Pull Requests 添加标签
  • 单元测试我们的行动
  • 在 Visual Studio Code 中调试
  • 将我们的操作发布到 GitHub Marketplace
  • 在另一个存储库中使用我们的操作
  • 一些最后的润色使我们的项目更健壮

如何使用本教程

这篇文章会比平时长得多，您可以跳过下面目录中的章节，但我建议您按照每个步骤进行操作，不要跳过任何内容。

另一个很好的建议是创建一个新的存储库并按照这些步骤进行操作。 通过这种方式，您将在帖子的末尾有一个有效的操作，您将能够使用它并进行实验，而不是仅仅阅读一篇很长的教程而忘记了大约 90% 的内容。

本教程的完整代码可在 GitHub 上的此 repo上找到，因此如果遇到困难，您可以随时参考它。

最后但并非最不重要的是，我还录制了本教程的视频版本，所以如果你更喜欢观看而不是阅读，你可以在这里这样做。

构建 MVP

让我们从 GitHub Action 的 MVP（最小可行产品）开始。

我们将从一个简单的操作开始，该操作仅包含了解基础知识所需的部分，然后我们将向其添加更多功能。

动作定义

该定义存储在action.yml文件中，直接放在存储库的根目录中。

 name: 'My Custom Action' description: 'Say Hello' author: 'Leonardo Montini' runs: using: 'node16' main: 'dist/index.js'

最小的动作定义由名称和runs对象组成，用于定义动作如何执行以及运行什么文件。 如果您的操作有一些输入，它们也将在inputs对象中的action.yml文件中定义。

 inputs: name: description: 'The name of the person to greet' required: true default: 'World'

将操作发布到商店时，您可能希望自定义操作在市场中的外观。 这可以通过将branding对象添加到action.yml文件来完成。

 branding: icon: 'activity' color: 'green'

无论如何，对于这个 MVP，第一个片段就足够了。

项目设置

让我们使用npm init -y创建一个空节点项目。

您现在可以打开package.json并添加构建脚本。

 { "scripts": { "build": "tsc" } }

由于我们想要使用 Typescript，因此我们需要将其安装为开发依赖项。

 npm install -D typescript

然后我们将通过运行npx tsc --init创建一个tsconfig.json并编辑它以满足我们的需要。

 { "compilerOptions": { "target": "ES2019", "module": "commonjs", "outDir": "lib", "esModuleInterop": true, "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "skipLibCheck": true } }

项目代码

我们现在可以创建src/index.ts文件并开始编写我们的操作。

 console.log('Hello World!');

我们正在构建一个 MVP，对吗？ 因此，让我们暂时保持简单并专注于所有必需的步骤。 以后有时间再扩充。

建立行动

我们现在可以通过运行npm run build来构建操作，这将执行我们在package.json文件中定义的tsc命令。

这将创建一个包含已编译代码的dist文件夹。

如果您看到在项目的根目录或src/中生成了index.js ，您可能需要添加确保tsconfig.json文件的outDir选项已正确设置为dist 。

创建存储库

如果您还没有这样做，是时候创建一个 git 存储库了，因为我们需要尽快将我们的代码推送到 GitHub。

不要忘记将.gitignore文件添加到项目中，以避免提交不应该被 git 跟踪的文件，例如node_modules文件夹。

一种快速的方法是运行npx gitignore node ，它将创建一个.gitignore文件，其中包含节点项目的最常见条目。

注意：在这种特殊情况下，我们希望将dist文件夹推送到 GitHub，因此我们需要从.gitignore文件中删除dist条目。

 git add . git commit -m "Initial commit" git remote add origin git push -u origin main

运行动作

我们都准备好了！ 是时候开始我们的行动了。

创建工作流

在 GitHub 上，Actions 由工作流执行。 工作流在存储在存储库的.github/workflows文件夹中的.yml文件中定义，因此让我们创建它。

 mkdir -p .github/workflows touch .github/workflows/greet.yml

文件的名称无关紧要，但最好给它起一个有意义的名称。

重要的是文件的内容，它定义了工作流程。 为简单起见，以下是您需要的最少说明。

 name: Say Hello on: workflow_dispatch: jobs: run-my-action: name: Checks if this action works runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - uses: ./

在这种情况下，该操作将签出当前存储库，然后运行我们刚刚创建的操作。 我们将./作为操作路径，因为我们想从当前存储库运行操作，但我们也可以使用不同的存储库。

它在我们之前创建的action.yml文件中定义。

还有一行值得on: workflow_dispatch:当我们从 Actions 选项卡手动触发它时，它告诉 GitHub 运行工作流。 如果您想了解更多信息，我录制了一个关于手动运行 GitHub Actions 的专门视频。

运行工作流

我们现在可以将我们的代码推送到 GitHub 并查看实际操作！

 git add . git commit -m "Add workflow" git push

推送代码后，我们可以转到“操作”选项卡并手动触发工作流。

工作流将开始运行，我们将在控制台中看到操作日志。

恭喜！ 您刚刚创建并运行了第一个 GitHub Action！

在本地运行操作

如果您想在本地运行操作，而不必每次都提交/推送，您可以使用act工具。

然后，您可以通过在存储库的根目录中执行act来运行操作。

您可以在我的视频中找到有关在本地运行 GitHub Actions 的更多信息。

读取输入

让我们通过从工作流中读取输入来使我们的操作更有趣。

将输入添加到操作定义

像往常一样，第一步是在action.yml文件中定义输入。

 inputs: name: description: 'The name of the person to greet' required: true default: 'World'

读取动作代码中的输入

然后我们可以读取操作代码中的输入。

 const name = getInput('name'); console.log(`Hello ${name}!`);

但是getInput函数是从哪里来的呢？ 它定义在我们需要安装的@actions/core包中。

 npm install @actions/core

然后在index.ts文件之上添加导入。

 import { getInput } from '@actions/core';

更新工作流程

最后，我们需要更新工作流以将输入传递给操作。

 - uses: ./ with: name: 'Leonardo'

通过添加with部分，我们将name输入传递给操作。

运行问候动作

我们现在可以再次运行操作并查看传递给操作的输入。

如果它不起作用（ Error: Cannot find module '@actions/core' ）没关系，我们将在下一节中将@vercel/ncc引入工作流时修复它。

用例：向新的拉取请求添加标签

我们学习了基础知识，我们的 MVP 已准备好进行扩展，所以让我们开始吧！ 我们将创建一个新操作，它会在每次创建新的合并请求时自动运行，并且会为其添加一个needs-review标签。

更新动作代码

为了给 Pull Request 添加标签，我们需要使用GitHub REST API 。 我们可以使用 Octokit 客户端进行 API 调用。 幸运的是，我们可以使用官方软件包。

 npm install @actions/github

然后我们可以导入Octokit客户端并使用它来将标签添加到 Pull Request 中。 这是完整的代码。

 import { getInput, setFailed } from '@actions/core'; import { context, getOctokit } from '@actions/github'; async function run() { const token = getInput('gh-token'); const label = getInput('label'); const octokit = getOctokit(token); const pullRequest = context.payload.pull_request; try { if (!pullRequest) { throw new Error('This action can only be run on Pull Requests'); } await octokit.rest.issues.addLabels({ owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: pullRequest.number, labels: [label], }); } catch (error) { setFailed((error as Error)?.message ?? 'Unknown error'); } } run();

这里发生了什么？

  1. 我们从工作流程中读取gh-token和label输入。 令牌是我们需要创建并传递给操作的个人访问令牌。 稍后会详细介绍。 我们使用令牌创建Octokit客户端的实例。
  2. 我们从context.payload.pull_request对象中获取 Pull Request 编号。 此对象仅在操作由拉取请求事件触发时可用。
  3. 我们调用octokit.rest.issues.addLabels方法将标签添加到 Pull Request。

万一出现问题，我们会捕获错误并使用setFailed函数将工作流设置为失败，该函数也来自@actions/core包。

更新动作定义

我们还需要更新操作定义以添加新输入。

 inputs: gh-token: description: 'The GitHub token for authentication.' required: true label: description: 'The label to be applied to the pull request.' required: true

更新工作流程

最后，我们需要更新工作流程以在打开或重新打开 PR 时运行：

 on: pull_request: types: [opened, reopened]

我们将GITHUB_TOKEN秘密传递给操作。 它通常不需要您执行任何额外操作，因为 GitHub 会自动为您创建它。

如果您收到“无法通过集成访问资源”的错误，您需要确保该存储库上的令牌具有写入权限。 您可以通过转到“设置”选项卡>“操作”>“常规”并向下滚动到“工作流程权限”部分来执行此操作。

将其设置为“读写权限”就足够了。

运行动作

我们现在可以再次运行该操作并看到标签已添加到合并请求中。

但是，我们不要忘记先运行npm run build ，然后我们才能提交并推送更改。 原因是我们告诉 GitHub 该操作位于dist文件夹中，因此我们需要确保该文件夹是最新的。

 npm run build git add . git commit -m "Add label to new Pull Requests" git push

凉爽的！ 我们现在可以创建一个新分支，更改一个文件并打开一个合并请求。 我们将看到操作正在运行并将标签添加到 Pull Request，aaaand...


 Error: Cannot find module '@actions/core'

这里发生了什么？ 如果您查看dist/index.js中的文件，您会发现@actions/core包不存在。 那是因为我们没有告诉 TypeScript 将它包含在构建中。

在一个文件中包含包和压缩所有内容的好方法是使用工具@vercel/ncc 。 让我们安装它。

然后我们可以更新package.json中的build脚本来使用它。

 "build": "tsc && ncc build lib/index.js"

我们还需要将tsconfig.json中的outDir更改为lib 。

您可能还想仔细检查lib是否在您的.gitignore文件中，但dist不在。 这是我们想要的行为，因为lib只会在 javascript 中包含我们的代码，但dist将具有整个操作。

运行动作 (2.0)

我们现在可以再次运行npm run build ，然后提交并推送更改。

创建一个新分支，更改一个文件并打开一个合并请求。 这一次，您将看到操作正在运行并将标签添加到合并请求。

测试动作

你不会在没有测试的情况下将代码投入生产，对吧？ 因此，让我们为我们的操作编写一些测试。

设置

我们将使用Jest来编写我们的测试。 它与 Javascript 开箱即用，但需要一些配置才能与 TypeScript 一起使用。

 npm install -D jest ts-jest @types/jest

我们还需要在项目的根目录中为 jest 创建一个新的配置文件，名为jest.config.json 。

 { "preset": "ts-jest", "testEnvironment": "node", "collectCoverage": true, "coverageReporters": ["lcov", "text-summary"], "collectCoverageFrom": ["src/**/*.ts"], "coveragePathIgnorePatterns": ["/node_modules/", "/__tests__/"], "testPathIgnorePatterns": ["/node_modules/"] }

我们告诉 jest 使用ts-jest预设，在节点中运行测试，收集覆盖率并忽略一些文件。

其他变化

没有任何额外的配置，构建还将包含我们不需要的测试文件。 在 tsconfig.json 上，我们可以添加一个新的exclude属性。

 "exclude": ["node_modules", "**/*.test.ts"]

而且，如果我们现在运行测试，一切都会运行两次。

为什么？

因为在index.ts文件的末尾，我们调用了run函数。 这是我们动作的入口点，我们希望在动作触发时运行它。 但是，当我们在测试中导入文件时，我们不希望它默认运行。

一个可能的解决方案是将对run的调用包装在一个if语句中，该语句检查是否设置了 jest 环境变量。

 if (!process.env.JEST_WORKER_ID) { run(); }

编写测试

我们现在可以在src/__tests__文件夹中创建一个名为index.test.ts的新文件。 这是我们将编写测试的地方。

我不得不说我有点懒，所以我请 GitHub Copilot 为我编写测试（猜猜是什么，我还制作了一个关于这个主题的视频）。 起初，他们没有通过，但经过一些调整后，我让他们通过了。 这是一个摘录，但您可以在存储库中找到完整的文件。

 import { run } from '../index'; import { getInput, setFailed } from '@actions/core'; import { context, getOctokit } from '@actions/github'; // Mock getInput and setFailed functions jest.mock('@actions/core', () => ({ getInput: jest.fn(), setFailed: jest.fn(), })); // Mock context and getOctokit functions jest.mock('@actions/github', () => ({ context: { payload: { pull_request: { number: 1, }, }, repo: { owner: 'owner', repo: 'repo', }, }, getOctokit: jest.fn(), })); describe('run', () => { beforeEach(() => { // Clear all mock function calls and reset mock implementation jest.clearAllMocks(); }); it('should add label to the pull request', async () => { // Mock the return values for getInput (getInput as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce('gh-token-value'); (getInput as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce('label-value'); (context as any).payload.pull_request = { number: 1, }; // Mock the Octokit instance and the addLabels method const mockAddLabels = jest.fn(); const mockOctokit = { rest: { issues: { addLabels: mockAddLabels, }, }, }; (getOctokit as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce(mockOctokit); // Run the function await run(); // Assertions expect(getInput).toHaveBeenCalledWith('gh-token'); expect(getInput).toHaveBeenCalledWith('label'); expect(getOctokit).toHaveBeenCalledWith('gh-token-value'); expect(mockAddLabels).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ owner: 'owner', repo: 'repo', issue_number: 1, labels: ['label-value'], }); expect(setFailed).not.toHaveBeenCalled(); }); });

运行测试

现在终于可以摆脱package.json中"test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1"脚本了。 只需将其替换为：

这将使用我们刚刚创建的配置运行 jest。 如果你想在 watch 模式下运行测试，你可以使用jest --watch 。

我们现在可以运行npm test来运行测试。 你应该看到这样的东西：

我们的行动按预期进行，我们有一些测试来证明这一点！

调试动作

我们最喜欢的工具是使用 console.log 充斥代码，但我们都知道有更好的调试代码的方法。 让我们看看如何使用 Visual Studio Code 调试我们的操作。

设置

首先，我们的开发依赖项中还需要一个包： ts-node 。 这将允许我们直接从源代码运行我们的动作，而不必先构建它。

使用 vscode，启动配置存储在一个名为launch.json的文件中。 我们可以通过单击侧边栏中的调试图标，然后单击齿轮图标来创建它，或者通过使用以下内容手动创建.vscode/launch.json文件来创建它：

 { "version": "0.2.0", "configurations": [ { "name": "ts-node", "type": "node", "request": "launch", "args": ["./src/index.ts"], "runtimeArgs": ["-r", "ts-node/register"], "cwd": "${workspaceRoot}", "internalConsoleOptions": "openOnSessionStart" } ] }

调试

您现在可以在代码中放置一个断点，然后单击调试侧边栏中的播放按钮。 这将启动调试器并运行您的操作。 您也可以使用F5键启动调试器。

在这个具体的例子中，没有太多需要调试的东西，但如果你正在处理一个更复杂的动作，这可能是一个救星。

输入

啊，我差点忘了，如果你的动作有一些输入呢？ 你怎么调试它？

您可以使用以下规则将它们作为环境变量直接存储在您的启动配置中，它们将被getInput获取：

  • 在输入名称前加上INPUT_
  • 以大写命名输入

在我们的示例中，它将是这样的：

 { "version": "0.2.0", "configurations": [ { "name": "ts-node", "type": "node", "request": "launch", "args": ["./src/index.ts"], "runtimeArgs": ["-r", "ts-node/register"], "cwd": "${workspaceRoot}", "internalConsoleOptions": "openOnSessionStart", "env": { "INPUT_GH-TOKEN": "secret-token-123456", "INPUT_LABEL": "needs-review" } } ] }

如果您现在按F5键启动调试器，您将看到环境变量在您的操作中可用，并且已被getInput正确读取。

发布动作

你们一直在等待的段落。 我敢肯定你也跳过了上一篇关于调试的文章直接跳到这一篇。

品牌推广

等一下，如果您还记得我们在action.yml文件中提到过branding部分。 让我们看看这是怎么回事。

 branding: icon: 'activity' color: 'green'

这将为您在 GitHub Marketplace 中的操作添加漂亮的图标和颜色。 您可以在此处找到可用图标的列表。

出版

好的，我们到了！

我们如何发布我们的行动？ 只需查看您的 GitHub 存储库即可。 如果您已正确设置action.yml文件，您应该会在页面顶部看到一个横幅，如下所示：

在 GitHub Marketplace 上线

单击Draft a release ，您将进入一个页面，您可以在其中设置所有必需/缺失的信息，例如，一个编写良好且信息丰富的README.md文件。

您可以决定是只想让每个人都可以使用该操作，还是还希望它在 GitHub Marketplace 中发布和发现。 您还可以从可用类别列表中选择主要类别和次要类别。

下一步将为您的操作创建标签和正式发布。

就快完成了……点击Publish release绿色按钮，你就完成了！

恭喜，您刚刚发布了第一个 GitHub Action！

使用你的行动

现在您已经发布了您的操作，每个人都可以在他们的工作流程中使用它！

只需在您的存储库中创建一个新文件，例如.github/workflows/label.yml并添加以下内容：

 name: Label PR on: pull_request: types: [opened, reopened] jobs: label: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@main - uses: Balastrong/gh-aciton-test@latest with: gh-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} label: needs-review

要指定版本，您可以使用owner/repo@{version} （ {version}是 repo 上的标记版本，如v1.0.0 ）或owner/repo@branch 。

最后润色

该操作已完成，它在商店中存在并且适用于所有 GitHub 存储库，但除非它 100% 完成并且您永远不会再次更新它，否则您仍有一些改进空间以获得更好的开发人员体验（DX 相当这些天的趋势）。

保持 dist 为最新

在此工作流程中，一件会让您烦恼的事情是推送新代码并忘记更新dist内容。

我的意思是，您所要做的就是确保在推送之前运行npm run build ，但是如果某些事情依赖于“只记得去做”......好吧......祝你好运！

解决这个问题的一个简单方法是使用 git hook。 您可以使用pre-commit挂钩在存储库中的每次提交时运行脚本。 这对你有用，但如果你和其他人一起工作怎么办？ 啊，请告诉他们也安装挂钩......我已经说过好运了吗？

一只狗？

是的，特别是哈士奇！

它的设置非常简单，它将确保克隆你的 repo 的每个人都将安装挂钩，因为它们是项目的一部分，并在运行npm install后立即与依赖项一起安装。 无需依赖人们记住安装它们！

让我们按照官方文档安装它：

 npx husky-init && npm install

您现在将在您的项目中找到一个.husky文件夹，其中包含一个pre-commit文件。 打开它并将内容替换为：

现在确保运行一次npm install来更新挂钩。 这将确保每次提交时， dist文件夹都会更新。

格式化和 linting

由于我们已经设置了pre-commit挂钩，我们还可以向其中添加一些格式化和 linting。

这将确保代码始终具有正确的格式并且没有 linting 错误。

让我们安装Prettier和ESLint ：

 npm install -D prettier eslint lint-staged

我们还需要为 ESLint 安装一些插件：

 npm install -D @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin@latest eslint-plugin-jest@latest

我们现在可以配置 eslint 和 prettier。 在项目的根目录中创建一个.eslintrc文件并添加以下内容：

 { "plugins": ["jest", "@typescript-eslint"], "parser": "@typescript-eslint/parser", "extends": ["eslint:recommended", "plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended"], "env": { "node": true, "jest/globals": true }, "ignorePatterns": ["*.test.ts"] }

我们还添加一个.prettierrc文件：

 { "trailingComma": "es5", "printWidth": 120, "tabWidth": 2 }

最后一点，我们只想在暂存文件上运行这两个文件，这意味着要多一个用于 lint-staged 的​​配置文件。 创建一个.lintstagedrc文件并添加以下内容：

 { "./src/**/*.{js,ts}": ["prettier --write", "eslint --max-warnings 0"] }

现在我们可以更新我们的pre-commit钩子来运行 lint-staged：

 npx husky add .husky/pre-commit "npx lint-staged"

再npm install ，我们就完成了！

关闭

如果您遵循了所有步骤，您现在就有了一个非常可靠的 GitHub Action，它发布在 GitHub Marketplace 上，它会在每次提交时自动构建和检查。 准备好做一个伟大的开源项目！

如果您想查看最终结果，可以查看我在撰写本文时创建的 GitHub Action 。 如果您错过了某些步骤，也可以将其用作参考。

想看我按照本文中的步骤进行操作吗？ 看看这个视频：

还有什么好说的？ 我希望你喜欢这篇文章并且你学到了一些新东西。 如果您有任何疑问，请随时在下方添加评论或在Twitter上与我联系。

这比平时要长得多，我在写它的过程中获得了很多乐趣，并且在这个过程中我自己也学到了很多东西。 你想要更多像这样的长篇文章吗？ 让我知道！

下次见！

感谢您阅读本文，希望您觉得它有趣！

我最近启动了我的 Discord 服务器来讨论开源和 Web 开发，欢迎加入： discord.gg/bqwyEa6We6

你喜欢我的内容吗？ 您可以考虑订阅我的 YouTube 频道！ 这对我来说意义重大❤️ 你可以在这里找到它：



原文:

GitHub Actions are a powerful tool to automate your workflow. They can be used to run tests, deploy your code, publish a package, and much more.

The cool thing is, there's a GitHub Actions Marketplace where you can find a lot of actions created by... the community. But what if you can't find the action you need? You can create your own and publish it there!

In this tutorial we're going to see in detail how to:

  • Create a GitHub Action in Typescript
  • Expand our Action to support custom inputs
  • Integrate with GitHub's API to add labels to Pull Requests
  • Unit testing our action
  • Debugging in Visual Studio Code
  • Publishing our action to the GitHub Marketplace
  • Using our action in another repository
  • Some final touches to make our project more robust

How to use this tutorial

This post will be much longer than usual, you can skip through the chapters from the table of contents below, but I'd recommend you to follow each step and not skip anything.

One more great piece of advice is to create a new repository and follow along with the steps. This way you'll have a working action at the end of the post and you'll be able to play with it and experiment, rather than just reading a long tutorial and forgetting about 90% of it.

The full code of this tutorial is available on GitHub on this repo, so you can always refer to it if you get stuck.

Last, but not least, I also recorded a video version of this tutorial, so if you prefer to watch it instead of reading, you can do so here.

Building an MVP

Let's begin with an MVP (Minimum Viable Product) of our GitHub Action.

We'll start simple with an action having only the required parts to understand the basics, then we'll add more features to it.

Action Definition

The definition is stored in the action.yml file, put directly in the root of the repository.

name: 'My Custom Action'
description: 'Say Hello'
author: 'Leonardo Montini' runs: using: 'node16' main: 'dist/index.js'

The most minimal action definition is composed of a name and a runs object to define how the action is executed and what file to run. description and author are optional.

In case your action has some inputs, they will be defined in the action.yml file as well, in the inputs object.

inputs: name: description: 'The name of the person to greet' required: true default: 'World'

When publishing the action to the store, you might want to customize the appearance of the action in the marketplace. This can be done by adding a branding object to the action.yml file.

branding: icon: 'activity' color: 'green'

Anyway, for this MVP the first snippet will be enough.

Project setup

Let's create an empty node project with npm init -y.

You can now open package.json and add the build script.

{ "scripts": { "build": "tsc" }
}

Since we want to use Typescript, we'll need to install it as a dev dependency.

npm install -D typescript

Then we'll create a tsconfig.json by running npx tsc --init and we'll edit it to match our needs.

{ "compilerOptions": { "target": "ES2019", "module": "commonjs", "outDir": "lib", "esModuleInterop": true, "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true, "strict": true, "skipLibCheck": true }
}

Project code

We can now create the src/index.ts file and start writing our action.

console.log('Hello World!');

We're building an MVP, right? So let's keep it simple for now and focus on all the required steps. We'll have time to expand it later.

Building the action

We can now build the action by running npm run build which will execute the tsc command as we defined in the package.json file.

This will create a dist folder containing the compiled code.

In case you see index.js generated in the root of the project or inside src/, you might want to add make sure the outDir option to the tsconfig.json file is properly set to dist.

Creating the repository

In case you haven't done it yet, it's time to create a git repository as we'll need to push our code to GitHub soon.

Don't forget to add a .gitignore file to the project to avoid committing files that shouldn't be tracked by git, for example, the node_modules folder.

A quick way to do it is by running npx gitignore node which will create a .gitignore file with the most common entries for a node project.

Note: in this particular case we want to push the dist folder to GitHub, so we'll need to remove the dist entry from the .gitignore file.

git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git remote add origin
git push -u origin main

Running the Action

We're all set! It's time to run our action.

Creating a workflow

On GitHub, Actions are executed by workflows. A workflow is defined in a .yml file stored in the .github/workflows folder of the repository so let's create it.

mkdir -p .github/workflows
touch .github/workflows/greet.yml

The name of the file doesn't matter, but it's good practice to give it a meaningful name.

What matters is the content of the file, which defines the workflow. To keep it simple, here are the minimal instructions you need.

name: Say Hello on: workflow_dispatch: jobs: run-my-action: name: Checks if this action works runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - uses: ./

In this case, the action will checkout the current repository and then run the action we just created. We put ./ as the action path because we want to run the action from the current repository, but we could also use a different repository.

How does GitHub know what action to run? It's defined in the action.yml file we created earlier.

One more line worth mentioning is on: workflow_dispatch: which tells GitHub to run the workflow when we manually trigger it from the Actions tab. I recorded a dedicated video about running GitHub Actions manually, if you want to learn more about it.

Running the workflow

We can now push our code to GitHub and see the action in action!

git add .
git commit -m "Add workflow"
git push

Once the code is pushed, we can go to the Actions tab and trigger the workflow manually.

The workflow will start running and we'll see the logs of the action in the console.

Congratulations! You just created and ran your first GitHub Action!

Running the action locally

In case you want to run the action locally, without having to commit/push every time, you can use the act tool.

Then you can run the action by executing act in the root of the repository.

You can find more in my video about running GitHub Actions locally.

Reading inputs

Let's make our action a bit more interesting by reading an input from the workflow.

Add inputs to the action definition

As usual, the first step is to define the input in the action.yml file.

inputs: name: description: 'The name of the person to greet' required: true default: 'World'

Read the input in the action code

Then we can read the input in the action code.

const name = getInput('name'); console.log(`Hello ${name}!`);

But where does the getInput function come from? It's defined in the @actions/core package, which we need to install.

npm install @actions/core

And then add the import on top of the index.ts file.

import { getInput } from '@actions/core';

Update the workflow

Finally, we need to update the workflow to pass the input to the action.

- uses: ./ with: name: 'Leonardo'

By adding the with section, we're passing the name input to the action.

Run the hello action

We can now run the action again and see the input being passed to the action.

If it doesn't work (Error: Cannot find module '@actions/core') that's ok, we'll fix it in the next section when we inluce @vercel/ncc in the workflow.

Use case: Adding a label to new Pull Requests

We learned the basics, we have our MVP ready to be expanded so let's do it! We'll create a new action that will automatically run every time a new Pull Request is created and it will add a needs-review label to it.

Update the action code

In order to add a label to a Pull Request, we need to use the GitHub REST API. We can use the Octokit client to make the API calls. Luckily for us there's an official package we can use.

npm install @actions/github

Then we can import the Octokit client and use it to add the label to the Pull Request. Here's the full code.

import { getInput, setFailed } from '@actions/core';
import { context, getOctokit } from '@actions/github'; async function run() { const token = getInput('gh-token'); const label = getInput('label'); const octokit = getOctokit(token); const pullRequest = context.payload.pull_request; try { if (!pullRequest) { throw new Error('This action can only be run on Pull Requests'); } await octokit.rest.issues.addLabels({ owner: context.repo.owner, repo: context.repo.repo, issue_number: pullRequest.number, labels: [label], }); } catch (error) { setFailed((error as Error)?.message ?? 'Unknown error'); }
} run();

What happened here?

  1. We read the gh-token and label inputs from the workflow. The token is a personal access token that we need to create and pass to the action. More on that later. The label is the name of the label we want to add to the Pull Request.
  2. We create an instance of the Octokit client using the token.
  3. We get the Pull Request number from the context.payload.pull_request object. This object is only available when the action is triggered by a Pull Request event.
  4. We call the octokit.rest.issues.addLabels method to add the label to the Pull Request.

In case something fails, we catch the error and we set the workflow as failed with the setFailed function, also coming from the @actions/core package.

Update the action definition

We also need to update the action definition to add the new inputs.

inputs: gh-token: description: 'The GitHub token for authentication.' required: true label: description: 'The label to be applied to the pull request.' required: true

Update the workflow

Finally, we need to update the workflow to run when PRs are opened or reopened:

on: pull_request: types: [opened, reopened]

...and to pass the new inputs to the action.

- uses: ./ with: gh-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} label: 'needs-review'

We're passing the GITHUB_TOKEN secret to the action. It usually does not require you any extra action, as GitHub automatically creates it for you.

In case you get an error that says "Resource not accessible by integration", you need to make sure your token on that repository has write access. You can do that by going to the Settings tab > Actions > General and scroll down to the "Workflow permissions" section.

Setting it to "Read and write permissions" will be enough.

Run the action

We can now run the action again and see the label is added to the Pull Request.

However, let's not forget to run npm run build first, then we can commit and push the changes. The reason is that we told GitHub that the action is located in the dist folder, so we need to make sure that folder is up to date.

npm run build
git add .
git commit -m "Add label to new Pull Requests"
git push

Cool! We can now create a new branch, change a file and open a Pull Request. We'll see the action running and adding the label to the Pull Request, aaaand...


Error: Cannot find module '@actions/core'

What happened here? If you look at your file in dist/index.js, you'll see that the @actions/core package is not there. That's because we didn't tell TypeScript to include it in the build.

A good way to include packages and condense everything in one file is with the tool @vercel/ncc. Let's install it.

Then we can update our build script in package.json to use it.

 "build": "tsc && ncc build lib/index.js"

We also need to change outDir in tsconfig.json to lib.

You might also want to double-check that lib is in your .gitignore file but dist is not. This is our desired behavior as lib will only have our code in javascript, but dist will have the whole action.

Run the action (2.0)

We can now run npm run build again, then commit and push the changes.

Create a new branch, change a file and open a Pull Request. This time, you'll see the action running and adding the label to the Pull Request.

Testing the action

You wouldn't push code into production without testing it, right? So let's write some tests for our action.

Setup

We'll use Jest to write our tests. It works out of the box with Javascript but needs a little bit of configuration to work with TypeScript.

npm install -D jest ts-jest @types/jest

We also need to create a new config file for jest, in the root of our project, called jest.config.json.

{ "preset": "ts-jest", "testEnvironment": "node", "collectCoverage": true, "coverageReporters": ["lcov", "text-summary"], "collectCoverageFrom": ["src/**/*.ts"], "coveragePathIgnorePatterns": ["/node_modules/", "/__tests__/"], "testPathIgnorePatterns": ["/node_modules/"]
}

We're telling jest to use the ts-jest preset, to run the tests in node, to collect coverage and to ignore some files.

Additional changes

Without any extra configuration, the build will also include test files, which we don't want. On tsconfig.json, we can add a new exclude property.

"exclude": ["node_modules", "**/*.test.ts"]

And also, if we'd run tests now, everything would run twice.

Why?

Because at the end of our index.ts file, we're calling the run function. This is the entry point of our action, and we want to run it when the action is triggered. However, we don't want it to run by default when we import the file in our tests.

A possible solution is to wrap the call to run in an if statement that checks if a jest environment variable is set.

if (!process.env.JEST_WORKER_ID) { run();
}

Writing the tests

We can now create a new file called index.test.ts in the src/__tests__ folder. This is where we'll write our tests.

I have to say I'm a bit lazy so I asked GitHub Copilot to write the tests for me (guess what, I also made a video on this topic). At first, they were not passing, but after a few tweaks, I got them to pass. Here's an extract, but you can find the full file in the repository.

import { run } from '../index';
import { getInput, setFailed } from '@actions/core';
import { context, getOctokit } from '@actions/github'; // Mock getInput and setFailed functions
jest.mock('@actions/core', () => ({ getInput: jest.fn(), setFailed: jest.fn(),
})); // Mock context and getOctokit functions
jest.mock('@actions/github', () => ({ context: { payload: { pull_request: { number: 1, }, }, repo: { owner: 'owner', repo: 'repo', }, }, getOctokit: jest.fn(),
})); describe('run', () => { beforeEach(() => { // Clear all mock function calls and reset mock implementation jest.clearAllMocks(); }); it('should add label to the pull request', async () => { // Mock the return values for getInput (getInput as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce('gh-token-value'); (getInput as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce('label-value'); (context as any).payload.pull_request = { number: 1, }; // Mock the Octokit instance and the addLabels method const mockAddLabels = jest.fn(); const mockOctokit = { rest: { issues: { addLabels: mockAddLabels, }, }, }; (getOctokit as jest.Mock).mockReturnValueOnce(mockOctokit); // Run the function await run(); // Assertions expect(getInput).toHaveBeenCalledWith('gh-token'); expect(getInput).toHaveBeenCalledWith('label'); expect(getOctokit).toHaveBeenCalledWith('gh-token-value'); expect(mockAddLabels).toHaveBeenCalledWith({ owner: 'owner', repo: 'repo', issue_number: 1, labels: ['label-value'], }); expect(setFailed).not.toHaveBeenCalled(); });
});

Running the tests

Now it's finally time to get rid of that "test": "echo \"Error: no test specified\" && exit 1" script in package.json. Just replace it with:

This will run jest with the configuration we just created. If you want to run the tests in watch mode, you can use jest --watch.

We can now run npm test to run the tests. You should see something like this:

Our action works as intended and we have some tests to prove it!

Debugging the action

Our favourite tool is flooding the code with console.log, but we all know there's a better way to debug code. Let's see how we can debug our action with Visual Studio Code.

Setup

To get started, we need one more package in our dev dependencies: ts-node. This will allow us to run our action directly from the source code, without having to build it first.

With vscode, the launch configuration is stored in a file called launch.json. We can create it by clicking on the debug icon in the sidebar and then on the gear icon, or manually by creating a .vscode/launch.json file with this content:

{ "version": "0.2.0", "configurations": [ { "name": "ts-node", "type": "node", "request": "launch", "args": ["./src/index.ts"], "runtimeArgs": ["-r", "ts-node/register"], "cwd": "${workspaceRoot}", "internalConsoleOptions": "openOnSessionStart" } ]
}

Debugging

You can now put a breakpoint in your code and click on the play button in the debug sidebar. This will start the debugger and run your action. You can also use the F5 key to start the debugger.

In this specific example, there isn't too much to debug, but if you're working on a more complex action, this can be a lifesaver.

Inputs

Ah, I almost forgot, what if your action has some inputs? How can you debug it?

You can store them directly in your launch configuration as environment variables with the following rules, the will get picked up by getInput:

  • prefix the name of the input with INPUT_
  • name the input in uppercase

In our example it would be something like this:

{ "version": "0.2.0", "configurations": [ { "name": "ts-node", "type": "node", "request": "launch", "args": ["./src/index.ts"], "runtimeArgs": ["-r", "ts-node/register"], "cwd": "${workspaceRoot}", "internalConsoleOptions": "openOnSessionStart", "env": { "INPUT_GH-TOKEN": "secret-token-123456", "INPUT_LABEL": "needs-review" } } ]
}

If you now hit F5 to start the debugger, you'll see that the environment variables are available in your action and are properly read by getInput.

Publishing the action

The paragraph you've all been waiting for. I'm sure you also skipped the previous one about debugging to jump straight to this one.

Branding

Hold on for a second, if you remember we mentioned the branding section in the action.yml file. Let's see what it's all about.

branding: icon: 'activity' color: 'green'

This will add a nice icon and a color to your action in the GitHub Marketplace. You can find the list of available icons here.

Publishing

Ok, here we are!

How do we publish our action? Just look at your GitHub repo. If you've properly set up an action.yml file, you should see a banner at the top of the page like this one:

Going live on the GitHub Marketplace

Click on Draft a release and you will get to a page where you can set up all the required/missing information, for example, a well-written and informative README.mdfile.

You can decide if you only want to make the action available to everyone, or you also want it to be published and discoverable in the GitHub Marketplace. You can also select a primary and a secondary category from the list of available ones.

The next step will be creating a tag and an official release for your action.

Almost there... click the Publish release green button and you're done!

Congratulations, you've just published your first GitHub Action!

Using your action

Now that you've published your action, everyone can use it in their workflows!

Just create a new file in your repo, for example .github/workflows/label.yml and add the following content:

name: Label PR on: pull_request: types: [opened, reopened] jobs: label: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@main - uses: Balastrong/gh-aciton-test@latest with: gh-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }} label: needs-review

To specify the version, you can either use owner/repo@{version} (with {version} being the tagged version on the repo, like v1.0.0) or owner/repo@branch.

Final touches

The action is complete, it's live in the store and it works on all GitHub repositories, but unless it's 100% complete and you won't ever update it again, you still have some room for improvements for a better developer experience (DX is quite trending these days).

Keeping dist up to date

One thing which will annoy you in this workflow is pushing new code and forgetting to also update the dist content.

I mean, all you have to do is just make sure to run npm run build before pushing, but if something relies on "just remember to do it"... well... good luck!

A simple way to solve this is to use a git hook. You can use the pre-commit hook to run a script at each commit in your repository. This will work for you, but what if you're working with other people? Ah, just kindly tell them to install the hook as well... did I already say good luck?

🐶

A Dog?

Yes, specifically a Husky!

Husky is a tool which allows you to easily add git hooks to your project. It's very easy to set up and it will make sure that everyone who clones your repo will have the hooks installed as well, as they are part of the project and get installed with the dependencies, right after running npm install. No need to rely on people remembering to install them!

Let's follow the official docs and install it:

npx husky-init && npm install

You will now find a .husky folder in your project, with a pre-commit file in it. Open it and replace the content with this:

Now make sure to run npm install once to update the hook. This will make sure that every time you commit, the dist folder will be updated.

Formatting & Linting

Since we already set up our pre-commit hook, we can also add some formatting and linting to it.

This will make sure that the code is always properly formatted and that there are no linting errors.

Let's install Prettier and ESLint:

npm install -D prettier eslint lint-staged

We will also need to install some plugins for ESLint:

npm install -D @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin@latest eslint-plugin-jest@latest

We can now configure eslint and prettier. Create a .eslintrc file in the root of your project and add the following content:

{ "plugins": ["jest", "@typescript-eslint"], "parser": "@typescript-eslint/parser", "extends": ["eslint:recommended", "plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended"], "env": { "node": true, "jest/globals": true }, "ignorePatterns": ["*.test.ts"]
}

Let's also add a .prettierrc file:

{ "trailingComma": "es5", "printWidth": 120, "tabWidth": 2
}

Last touch, we want to run these two only on the staged files, which means one more config file for lint-staged. Create a .lintstagedrc file and add the following content:

{ "./src/**/*.{js,ts}": ["prettier --write", "eslint --max-warnings 0"]
}

Now we can update our pre-commit hook to run lint-staged as well:

npx husky add .husky/pre-commit "npx lint-staged"

One more npm install and we're done!

Closing

If you followed all steps, you now have a pretty solid GitHub Action, published in the GitHub Marketplace, which is automatically built and linted on each commit. Ready to make a great Open Source project!

If you want to see the final result, you can check out the GitHub Action I created while writing this article. You can also use it as a reference if you missed some steps.

Want to see me following along the steps in this article? Check out the video here:

What else to say? I hope you enjoyed this article and that you learned something new. If you have any questions, feel free to add a comment below or reach out to me on Twitter.

This was much longer than usual, I had a lot of fun writing it and I learned a lot myself in the process. Would you like more long articles like this one? Let me know!

See you next time!

Thanks for reading this article, I hope you found it interesting!

I recently launched my Discord server to talk about Open Source and Web Development, feel free to join: discord.gg/bqwyEa6We6

Do you like my content? You might consider subscribing to my YouTube channel! It means a lot to me ❤️ You can find it here:
item.author: balastrong

item.title: Come hoje 研究网络开发 | Comece hoje a estudar web development
item.description:
奥德尔索的乔瓦尼·阿尔维斯

发表于 6 月 11 日

前端：

  • 控制 Git 的基本命令，包括git init 、 git add 、 git commit和git push 、 além de entender os conceitos de branches (ramificações) 和 pull requests。

前端框架：

  • 后端：

    • 研究基本语法、公式操作、银行间交互和 recursos avançados disponíveis nas versões mis recentes do PHP。

    • 集中在 MySQL 银行的基础上，使用 MySQL 来管理表格、顾问 dados、atualizar registros 和 executar operações básicas。

    • 控制 Git 的基本命令，包括git init 、 git add 、 git commit和git push 、 além de entender os conceitos de branches (ramificações) 和 pull requests。

    • 互联网：Uma conexão à internet é essencial para acessar recursos, documentação, tutoriais e participar de comunidades de programadores。 使用一个 para se conectar com outras pessoas na area e expandir seu conhecimento。

    利用 os recursos disponíveis，estude intensamente e mergulhe fundo no campo escolhido。 一旦暂停，oderco 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

解除暂停后，oderco 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，oderco 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 oderco 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 oderco：

降低 oderco 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

不屈不挠的 oderco 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Giovanni Alves for Oderço

Posted on Jun 11

 

Você não precisa ter o melhor computador, melhor curso ou de faculdade para começar. Aqui vou listar um roadmap para iniciantes.

Front-end:

  • HTML5: Comece aprendendo a estrutura básica de uma página web usando tags HTML, como <html>, <head>, <body>, e aprenda a criar elementos como cabeçalhos, parágrafos, links, imagens e formulários.

  • CSS3: Aprenda a estilizar suas páginas HTML usando CSS. Estude seletores, propriedades e valores CSS para alterar cores, fontes, tamanhos e posicionamento dos elementos na página.

  • Flexbox: Domine o conceito de flexbox, que é uma técnica de layout responsivo do CSS3. Isso permitirá que você crie layouts flexíveis e fluidos para seus projetos.

  • JavaScript: Familiarize-se com a linguagem de programação JavaScript, que é usada para adicionar interatividade e funcionalidades dinâmicas às páginas web. Aprenda os fundamentos, como variáveis, estruturas de controle (condicionais, loops) e funções.

  • ES5 e ES6: Estude as versões do JavaScript, ES5 e ES6 (também conhecido como ES2015). ES6 introduz muitos recursos avançados e melhorias na linguagem, como classes, arrow functions, operadores spread, desestruturação e muito mais.

  • Git & GitHub: Aprenda a usar o Git, um sistema de controle de versão amplamente utilizado, para acompanhar e gerenciar o histórico de alterações em seu código. Em seguida, familiarize-se com o GitHub, uma plataforma online para hospedar repositórios Git, colaborar em projetos, gerenciar problemas e solicitações de pull. Domine os comandos básicos do Git, como git init, git add, git commit e git push, além de entender os conceitos de branches (ramificações) e pull requests.

Frameworks para front-end:

  • Vite: Aprenda a configurar e usar o Vite, um framework de desenvolvimento rápido para projetos front-end. Ele oferece uma inicialização rápida, recarregamento instantâneo do navegador e suporte a módulos ES.

  • React, Vue, Angular (opcional): Escolha um dos principais frameworks JavaScript, como React, Vue ou Angular, e aprenda a criar aplicativos front-end escaláveis e reativos usando essas ferramentas.

  • Básico de linux/bash

Back-end:

  • PHP7 e PHP8: Comece aprendendo PHP, uma linguagem de script do lado do servidor amplamente usada para desenvolvimento web. Estude a sintaxe básica, manipulação de formulários, interação com banco de dados e recursos avançados disponíveis nas versões mais recentes do PHP.

  • MySQL: Aprenda o básico de bancos de dados relacionais e SQL (Structured Query Language). Concentre-se em aprender a criar tabelas, consultar dados, atualizar registros e executar operações básicas de banco de dados usando MySQL.

  • Laravel: Explore o framework Laravel, um dos mais populares e poderosos para desenvolvimento PHP. Estude os conceitos básicos, como roteamento, controle de versão, migrations e Eloquent ORM (Object-Relational Mapping).

  • Apache2 e Nginx: Familiarize-se com servidores web como o Apache2 e o Nginx. Aprenda a configurar virtual hosts, lidar com regras de redirecionamento e otimizar o desempenho do servidor.

  • Git & GitHub: Aprenda a usar o Git, um sistema de controle de versão amplamente utilizado, para acompanhar e gerenciar o histórico de alterações em seu código. Em seguida, familiarize-se com o GitHub, uma plataforma online para hospedar repositórios Git, colaborar em projetos, gerenciar problemas e solicitações de pull. Domine os comandos básicos do Git, como git init, git add, git commit e git push, além de entender os conceitos de branches (ramificações) e pull requests.

  • Básico de linux/bash

Você não precisa aprender os dois para entrar na área, você precisa escolher um e estudar muito. Não existe uma fórmula mágica, bootcamp ou qualquer outro recurso que garantirá um emprego imediato. Se você tem vontade e determinação, estude profundamente o campo escolhido.

Mas e se você não tem um computador bom? Não se preocupe, você pode começar mesmo com um Core 2 Duo e 4GB de RAM. Este roadmap foi desenvolvido levando em consideração essa limitação e você ainda poderá progredir significativamente.

Aqui estão algumas ferramentas leves que você pode utilizar:

  • Sublime Text 4: Um editor de texto leve e versátil, que oferece uma ótima experiência de programação. É uma excelente opção para começar a escrever código.

  • Windows 7+ ou qualquer distribuição Linux: Ambos os sistemas operacionais são adequados para desenvolvimento. O Windows 7 ou uma versão mais recente é uma escolha comum, mas também existem diversas distribuições Linux leves e amigáveis que você pode experimentar.

  • Internet: Uma conexão à internet é essencial para acessar recursos, documentação, tutoriais e participar de comunidades de programadores. Utilize-a para se conectar com outras pessoas na área e expandir seu conhecimento.

Lembre-se de que o hardware não é o único fator determinante para o sucesso na programação. O que importa mais é a sua dedicação e vontade de aprender. Utilize os recursos disponíveis, estude intensamente e mergulhe fundo no campo escolhido. Com perseverança, você poderá alcançar seus objetivos, mesmo com um computador mais modesto.

Daniel Camucatto - Jun 11

Daria Gorchylina - Jun 11

Once suspended, oderco will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, oderco will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by oderco will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If oderco is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Note:

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag oderco:

Make all posts by oderco less visible

oderco consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging oderco will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: gdois

item.title: 利用结对编程的力量：一起编码的艺术 | Harnessing the Power of Pair Programming: The Art of Coding Together
item.description:
在创新和效率至上的软件开发世界中，出现了一种动态方法，它动摇了传统编程实践的基础。 输入结对编程 - 激发创造力、增强问题解决能力并彻底改变代码编写方式的协作奇迹。 与我们一起踏上令人振奋的旅程，揭开结对编程的力量，两个人的思想团结起来产生非凡的结果。

在这个充满复杂编码挑战的时代，结对编程是协作的灯塔。 它打破了程序员的单独监禁，相信团队合作是取得非凡成就的催化剂。 通过结合两个人的独特观点、专业知识和经验，结对编程为创新铺平了道路，并将卓越推向了新的高度。

孤立的编码马拉松式的日子已经一去不复返了，在这种情况下，单独的开发人员将独自解决复杂的问题。 结对编程开创了共享探索的新时代，程序员成为同志，实时无缝协作以产生优雅的解决方案。 想象一下在一个工作站上和谐演奏的思想交响乐，每一行代码都由两个充满激情的头脑而不是一个人仔细检查、打磨和完善。

但结对编程到底是什么？ 它不仅仅是一种技术； 这是一种颂扬合作艺术的哲学。 想象两个角色交织在一起：熟练地操纵键盘、将想法转化为代码的“司机”，以及充当守护天使、提供指导、提出问题和挑战假设的“观察者”或“领航员”。 他们一起踏上了通往卓越的共同旅程，不断交换见解并互相激励以充分发挥潜力。

在本文中，我们将深入探讨结对编程的核心，探索它的好处，揭开它的挑战，并发现成功实施的秘诀。 系好安全带，因为这次令人振奋的航程有望永远改变您对软件开发的看法。 让我们一起通过结对编程解锁未开发的代码协同作用。

请记住，伟大的思想相似，但最伟大的思想协作。 欢迎来到结对编程的世界。

结对编程的工作原理：

结对编程涉及两个基本角色：

让我们探讨这些角色、结对编程会话的典型流程以及有效沟通和协作的重要性。

驱动程序：驱动程序是主动编写代码的人。 他们可以控制键盘和鼠标，实施解决方案并将想法转化为代码。 驱动程序负责执行编码任务并确保代码的正确性和功能性。

  • 观察者：观察者，也称为领航员，起到辅助作用。 他们积极与司机互动，审查编写的每一行代码。 观察员的职责包括提供反馈、提出澄清问题、提出改进建议以及考虑替代方法。 观察者的新鲜视角有助于发现错误并鼓励批判性思维。

    • 计划：在开始之前，两人讨论手头的任务或问题，定义目标和设定期望。 他们创建了对项目需求的共同理解并概述了方法。

  • 迭代开发：两人一起工作，驱动程序编写代码，观察者积极参与该过程。 他们不断地讨论想法、做出设计决策并协作解决问题。 观察者及时反馈，保证代码质量，促进高效解决方案。
  • 转换角色：为了保持平衡和防止疲劳，两人经常定期轮换角色，通常是每 30 分钟到一个小时。 驾驶员成为观察者，反之亦然。 这种轮换促进了知识共享，并使双方都参与并参与了该过程。
  • 持续沟通：在整个会议期间，驾驶员和观察员进行公开有效的沟通。 他们讨论自己的想法，分享见解，并共同努力克服挑战。 这种持续不断的对话促进了富有成效的协作环境。

    • 错误检测和代码质量：两只眼睛仔细检查代码可显着降低漏掉错误的机会。 观察者可以发现错误、提供即时反馈并提出改进建议，从而产生更高质量的代码。

  • 知识共享：结对编程促进了团队成员之间的知识转移。 初级程序员可以向更有经验的同事学习，获得新技能和最佳实践。 同样，高级程序员从他们的合作伙伴那里获得了新的观点和替代方法。
  • 解决问题和创新：协作鼓励不同的观点并激发创造力。 通过积极的讨论，结对可以探索不同的解决方案、挑战假设，并发现以前可能没有考虑过的创新方法。
  • 团队凝聚力和学习：结对编程培养友情并增强团队动力。 它促进信任，鼓励协作，并提高团队成员之间的沟通技巧。 它还为个人提供了一个相互学习并作为一个团队共同成长的机会。

    • 有效的沟通和协作构成了成功结对编程的基础。 定期和公开的对话、积极参与和支持性的环境确保了最好的结果。 通过利用驱动程序和观察程序的综合优势，结对编程最大限度地提高了代码质量，促进了知识共享，并促进了软件开发项目中的创新问题解决。

    结对编程的好处：

    • 提高代码质量：两组眼睛捕捉错误并提高整体代码质量。
    • 持续的知识共享：知识在团队成员之间传递，减少知识孤岛。
    • 减少调试时间：尽早发现错误，最大限度地减少调试工作。
    • 改进设计和解决问题：不同的观点会带来更好的解决方案。
    • 增加团队凝聚力：结对编程促进团队合作。
    • 入职和技能发展：初级程序员可以向经验丰富的队友学习。

    挑战与解决方案：

    与任何协作努力一样，结对编程也有其困难。 为确保无缝有效的配对体验，采取积极措施应对这些挑战至关重要。 以下是结对编程中可能出现的一些常见挑战以及克服这些挑战的建议解决方案：

    观点挑战解决方案
    人格冲突性格和工作方式的差异技能水平差异不同的技能水平导致贡献不平衡沟通与协作沟通差距和误解疲劳和倦怠长时间的训练导致身心疲惫缺乏所有权或自主权感知到的所有权和自主权减少远程结对编程技术壁垒和沟通困难通过使用建议的解决方案应对这些挑战，团队可以克服障碍并最大限度地发挥结对编程的优势。

    成功实施：

    有效的结对编程实现需要考虑各种因素并采用最佳实践。 为成功实施，请记住以下几点：

    在信任、尊重和开放沟通的基础上培养积极的团队文化。

  • 鼓励协作并创造一个让个人能够轻松分享想法和寻求帮助的环境。
  • 在团队成员之间建立牢固的关系，以促进有效的结对和知识共享。

    • 为每个结对编程会议设定明确的目标和期望。 定义要解决的问题、期望的结果以及要完成的任何特定任务。

  • 清楚地传达项目的范围和优先级，以确保配对之间的一致性。
  • 定期审查和重新评估目标以确保进展和适应性。

    • 定期轮流结对，以促进知识共享、避免疲劳并鼓励不同观点。

  • 与不同的人结对会使开发人员接触到不同的工作方式、方法和技能组合，从而促进持续学习和技能发展。

    • 鼓励结对之间公开和频繁的交流。 讨论想法、提出问题并提供建设性反馈。

  • 积极倾听和同理心对于理解和解决每个团队成员的观点和关注点至关重要。
  • 利用屏幕共享、代码审查和共享文档等协作工具和技术来加强沟通和理解。

    • 鼓励团队成员的成长心态，重视持续学习和改进。

  • 进行回顾以反思结对编程经验，确定需要改进的领域，并相应地实施更改。
  • 在团队内分享经验教训和最佳实践，以提高结对编程的有效性。

    • 创造一个支持性的环境，让个人感到安全，可以冒险、犯错并从中吸取教训。

  • 提供资源和支持，帮助开发人员提高技能和知识。
  • 庆祝成就并承认每个团队成员的贡献，以营造积极和激励的氛围。

    • 真实世界的例子：

    结对编程已经被很多企业和团队使用，并且产生了显着的优势。 以下是一些成功案例：

    • 重点介绍受益于结对编程的公司或团队的成功案例。
      结对编程帮助 IBM 团队交付质量更高、缺陷更少的软件并缩短了上市时间。 据 IBM 称，结对编程有助于提高 15% 到 20% 的生产力，并将代码质量提高 20% 到 30%。

      微软：

    结对编程帮助 GitHub 团队更有效地识别和修复问题，从而提高代码质量并更快地交付可靠的服务。

    关键实验室：

    • 他们将自己的成功归功于结对编程，结对编程促进了持续协作、知识共享和代码质量。 结论：

      结对编程在软件开发中提供了许多好处，例如提高代码质量、减少缺陷、有效沟通和提高生产力。 然而，必须解决诸如性格冲突和技能水平差异等潜在挑战。
      通过解决这些挑战并实施最佳实践，团队可以克服障碍并获得结对编程的回报。 随着软件开发的不断发展，我鼓励招聘人员考虑在他们的流程中探索或实施结对编程，以释放其潜在好处并推动他们的项目取得成功。

      快乐黑客！

      扁豆在这里🚀
      作为开发人员，您更喜欢在项目中结对吗？ 在评论区让我知道你的想法

      请喜欢，分享并关注我们以获取更多信息。

      一旦暂停，qbentil 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被删除。

      解除暂停后，qbentil 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

      一旦取消发布，qbentil 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

      如果 qbentil 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

      笔记：

      感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 qbentil：

      使 qbentil 的所有帖子不那么显眼

      报告其他不当行为

      取消标记 qbentil 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



      原文:

      In the world of software development, where innovation and efficiency reign supreme, a dynamic approach has emerged, shaking the foundations of conventional programming practices. Enter pair programming – the collaborative marvel that ignites creativity, enhances problem-solving, and revolutionizes the way code is crafted. Join us on an exhilarating journey as we unveil the power of pair programming, where two minds unite to produce exceptional results.

      In this era of complex coding challenges, pair programming stands tall as a beacon of collaboration. It breaks the solitary confinement of programmers, embracing the belief that teamwork is the catalyst for extraordinary achievements. By combining the unique perspectives, expertise, and experiences of two individuals, pair programming paves the way for innovation and drives excellence to new heights.

      Gone are the days of isolated coding marathons, where lone developers would grapple with intricate problems on their own. Pair programming ushers in a new era of shared exploration, where programmers become comrades, seamlessly collaborating in real-time to produce elegant solutions. Imagine a symphony of ideas harmoniously playing out on a single workstation, where each line of code is scrutinized, polished, and refined by not one, but two passionate minds.

      But what exactly is pair programming? It's more than just a technique; it's a philosophy that celebrates the art of collaboration. Picture two roles intertwined: the "driver" who skillfully navigates the keyboard, translating ideas into code, and the "observer" or "navigator" who acts as the guardian angel, offering guidance, asking questions, and challenging assumptions. Together, they embark on a mutual journey towards excellence, constantly exchanging insights and inspiring each other to reach their full potential.

      In this article, we will dive deep into the heart of pair programming, exploring its benefits, unraveling its challenges, and discovering the secrets to successful implementation. Buckle up, for this exhilarating voyage promises to forever transform the way you perceive software development. Together, let's unlock the untapped synergy of code through pair programming.

      Remember, great minds think alike, but the greatest minds collaborate. Welcome to the world of pair programming.

      How Pair Programming Works:

      Pair programming involves two essential roles:

      Let's explore these roles, the typical flow of pair programming sessions, and the importance of effective communication and collaboration.

      1. Roles of the Driver and Observer:

      • The Driver: The driver is the person actively writing the code. They have control of the keyboard and mouse, implementing the solutions and translating ideas into code. The driver is responsible for executing the coding tasks and ensuring the code's correctness and functionality.
      • The Observer: The observer, also known as the navigator, plays a supportive role. They actively engage with the driver, reviewing each line of code being written. The observer's responsibilities include providing feedback, asking clarifying questions, suggesting improvements, and considering alternative approaches. The observer's fresh perspective helps catch errors and encourages critical thinking.

      2. Typical Flow of Pair Programming Sessions:

      • Planning: Before starting, the pair discusses the task or problem at hand, defining goals and setting expectations. They create a shared understanding of the project's requirements and outline an approach.
      • Iterative Development: The pair works together, with the driver writing the code and the observer actively participating in the process. They continuously discuss ideas, make design decisions, and solve problems collaboratively. The observer provides timely feedback, ensuring code quality and promoting efficient solutions.
      • Switching Roles: To maintain balance and prevent fatigue, pairs often rotate roles at regular intervals, typically every 30 minutes to an hour. The driver becomes the observer, and vice versa. This rotation promotes knowledge sharing and keeps both individuals engaged and involved in the process.
      • Continuous Communication: Throughout the session, the driver and observer engage in open and effective communication. They discuss their thoughts, share insights, and work together to overcome challenges. This constant dialogue fosters a productive and collaborative environment.

      3. Importance of Effective Communication and Collaboration:

      • Error Detection and Code Quality: Two sets of eyes scrutinizing the code significantly reduce the chances of errors slipping through. The observer can catch mistakes, provide immediate feedback, and suggest improvements, resulting in higher-quality code.
      • Knowledge Sharing: Pair programming facilitates the transfer of knowledge between team members. Junior programmers can learn from more experienced colleagues, acquiring new skills and best practices. Similarly, senior programmers gain fresh perspectives and alternative approaches from their partners.
      • Problem Solving and Innovation: Collaboration encourages diverse viewpoints and sparks creativity. Through active discussion, pairs can explore different solutions, challenge assumptions, and uncover innovative approaches that might not have been considered otherwise.
      • Team Cohesion and Learning: Pair programming cultivates a sense of camaraderie and strengthens team dynamics. It promotes trust, encourages collaboration, and improves communication skills among team members. It also provides an opportunity for individuals to learn from one another and grow together as a team.

      Effective communication and collaboration forms the basis successful pair programming. Regular and open dialogue, active participation, and a supportive environment ensure the best possible outcomes. By leveraging the combined strengths of the driver and observer, pair programming maximizes code quality, fosters knowledge sharing, and promotes innovative problem-solving in software development projects.

      Benefits of Pair Programming:

      • Enhanced code quality: Two sets of eyes catch errors and improve overall code quality.
      • Continuous knowledge sharing: Knowledge is transferred between team members, reducing knowledge silos.
      • Reduced debugging time: Bugs are caught early, minimizing debugging efforts.
      • Improved design and problem-solving: Different perspectives lead to better solutions.
      • Increased team cohesion: Pair programming fosters teamwork and collaboration.
      • Onboarding and skill development: Junior programmers can learn from experienced teammates.

      Challenges and Solutions:

      Like any collaborative endeavor, pair programming has its share of difficulties. To ensure a seamless and effective pairing experience, it's crucial to take proactive measures to address these challenges. Here are some common challenges that may arise in pair programming and suggested solutions to overcome them:

      Point Challenge Solution
      Personality Conflicts Differences in personalities and working styles - Foster an inclusive team culture- Promote empathy

      - Encourage open discussions
      Skill Level Differences Varying skill levels leading to imbalanced contributions - Create a supportive environment for knowledge sharing
      - Encourage mentorship and rotation
      Communication and Collaboration Communication gaps and misunderstandings - Encourage clear and concise communication
      - Regular check-ins and documentation
      Fatigue and Burnout Prolonged sessions leading to mental and physical exhaustion - Set realistic time limits and encourage breaks
      - Rotate roles to prevent burnout
      Lack of Ownership or Autonomy Reduced perceived ownership and autonomy - Emphasize collective code ownership and shared goals
      - Provide opportunities for autonomy
      Remote Pair Programming Technological barriers and communication difficulties - Utilize remote collaboration tools
      - Establish clear communication channels

      By addressing these challenges with the suggested solutions, teams can overcome obstacles and maximize the benefits of pair programming.

      Successful Implementation:

      Effective pair programming implementation calls for consideration of various factors and the adoption of best practices. For successful implementation, keep in mind the following:

      1. Team Dynamics and Trust:

      • Foster a positive team culture based on trust, respect, and open communication.
      • Encourage collaboration and create an environment where individuals feel comfortable sharing ideas and asking for help.
      • Build strong relationships among team members to facilitate effective pairing and knowledge sharing.

      2. Clear Goals and Expectations:

      • Set clear goals and expectations for each pair programming session. Define the problem to be solved, desired outcomes, and any specific tasks to be accomplished.
      • Clearly communicate the scope and priorities of the project to ensure alignment between pairs.
      • Regularly review and reassess goals to ensure progress and adaptability.

      3. Rotation of Pairs:

      • Rotate pairs regularly to promote knowledge sharing, avoid fatigue, and encourage diverse perspectives.
      • Pairing with different individuals exposes developers to different working styles, approaches, and skill sets, facilitating continuous learning and skill development.

      4. Effective Communication:

      • Encourage open and frequent communication between pairs. Discuss ideas, ask questions, and provide constructive feedback.
      • Active listening and empathy are crucial for understanding and addressing the viewpoints and concerns of each team member.
      • Utilize collaboration tools and techniques, such as screen sharing, code reviews, and shared documentation, to enhance communication and understanding.

      5. Continuous Learning and Improvement:

      • Encourage a growth mindset among team members, valuing continuous learning and improvement.
      • Conduct retrospectives to reflect on pair programming experiences, identify areas for improvement, and implement changes accordingly.
      • Share lessons learned and best practices within the team to enhance the effectiveness of pair programming.

      6. Supportive Environment:

      • Create a supportive environment where individuals feel safe to take risks, make mistakes, and learn from them.
      • Provide resources and support to help developers enhance their skills and knowledge.
      • Celebrate achievements and acknowledge the contributions of each team member to foster a positive and motivating atmosphere.

      Real-World Examples:

      Pair programming has been used by many businesses and teams, and it has produced significant advantages. Here are a few success stories:

      • Highlight success stories of companies or teams that have benefited from pair programming.
        IBM:

      • IBM's: Extreme Blue program, which focuses on innovation and product development, extensively uses pair programming. Pair programming helped IBM teams deliver higher-quality software with fewer defects and reduced time-to-market. According to IBM, pair programming contributed to a 15% to 20% increase in productivity and improved code quality by 20% to 30%.

        Microsoft:

      GitHub has embraced pair programming in various development projects.
      Pair programming helped GitHub teams identify and fix issues more efficiently, resulting in improved code quality and faster delivery of reliable service.

      Pivotal Labs:

      • Pivotal Labs, a renowned software consulting company, has been a strong advocate of pair programming for years. They attribute their success to pair programming, which promotes continuous collaboration, knowledge sharing, and code quality. Pivotal Labs found that pair programming increased code readability, reduced defects, and improved team communication and cohesion.

      Conclusion:

      Pair programming offers many benefits in software development, such as enhanced code quality, reduced defects, effective communication, and improved productivity. However, potential challenges such as personality conflicts and skill level differences must be addressed.
      By addressing these challenges and implementing best practices, teams can overcome obstacles and reap the rewards of pair programming. As software development continues to evolve, I encourage recruiters to consider exploring or implementing pair programming in their processes to unlock its potential benefits and drive successful outcomes in their projects.

      Happy Hacking!

      Bentil here🚀
      Do you prefer being paired on a project as a developer? Let me know your thoughts in the comment section

      Kindly Like, Share and follow us for more.

      Unflagging qbentil will restore default visibility to their posts.
item.author: qbentil

item.title: 什么是 DevOps | What is DevOps
item.description:
在我们掌握 DevOps 的 120 天旅程的第一天，我们的目标是通过透彻理解支持 DevOps 思维的原则和工具来打下坚实的基础。

然而，在 2023 年 3 月，一个难得的机会出现了，我发现自己被 Qwasar 的云工程战略所吸引，重点是 Kubernetes、AWS 和 DevOps 文化。

当我深入研究这个新的学习之旅时，我很快意识到 DevOps 的意义比我最初想象的要多得多。 我对这个职业的新热情并不仅仅是因为网络开发而受到经济利益的驱动。 通过与该领域的导师和专家的互动，我获得了有关支持 DevOps 实践的各种工具、流程和文化价值观的宝贵知识。

为了确保我充分利用这一激动人心的旅程并与他人分享我的见解，我决定公开记录我感兴趣的领域和进展。

我们的探险开始了

我创建了一个名为 Engineers Group 的充满活力和多元化的社区，由来自软件工程、项目管理、网络工程等各个领域的专业人士和积极进取的学生组成。 我们的主要目标是学习和实施 DevOps 和云实践。 作为此旅程的一部分，我的目标是通过全面涵盖所有基础领域来记录我们在未来 120 天的进展。 此外，我鼓励社区的所有成员通过分享他们的经验和资源来积极贡献，以促进协作学习。

为了开启这一激动人心的旅程，我们将在前六天探索 DevOps 的基础知识，然后在接下来的 16 周内深入研究特定领域。 我还建议每个人都创建一个个人思维导图来确定自己的兴趣并相应地定位自己。

此外，我想分享另一个我认为每个人都应该探索的宝贵资源，以增强他们对 DevOps 的理解。 如下所示：
开发运营路线图

分解 DevOps：关键概念和原则

当我们开始学习DevOps 的 120 天挑战时，了解它是什么以及它为什么重要是很重要的。 虽然网上有无数可用资源，但我提炼了一些高级概念，它们将成为我们旅程的一个很好的起点。

首先，请务必注意 DevOps 不是您可以购买或下载的工具。 它既不是一种编程语言，也不是某种神秘的艺术。 相反，它是一种在软件开发中更有效地做事的方法。

尽管名称如此，DevOps 不仅适用于开发人员 - 任何参与软件开发的人，从运营到 QA 工程师，都将从了解它中受益。 通过采用 DevOps 思维方式，团队可以从冗长、冗长的发布周期转变为更频繁、更小的发布。

两者经常一起使用，以实现应用程序的持续交付。

通过将所有人聚集在一起并鼓励协作，DevOps 可以帮助简化整个开发过程。

最后，自动化在使 DevOps 有效和高效方面发挥着关键作用。 通过尽可能自动化任务，团队可以减少错误并加快整个过程。

资源

我总是愿意添加额外的资源。

我的建议是观看以下所有内容，希望您也从上面的文本和解释中学到了一些东西。

感谢大家探索我对第 1 天的了解希望明天在第 2 天见到你们



原文:

On day one of our 120-day journey towards mastering DevOps, we aim to lay a strong foundation by gaining a thorough understanding of the principles and tools that support the DevOps mindset.

I began my foray into the world of tech 16 months ago at ALX Software Engineering School, where I focused on Web Development. However, in March 2023, a remarkable opportunity presented itself, and I found myself drawn to Cloud Engineering strategy at Qwasar, with an emphasis on Kubernetes, AWS, and DevOps culture.

As I delved deeper into this new learning journey, I quickly realized that there was much more to DevOps than I had initially thought. My newfound passion for this career was not solely driven by financial gain as it was with Web Development. Through interactions with mentors and experts in the field, I gained invaluable knowledge on the diverse tooling, processes, and cultural values that underpin DevOps practices.

To ensure that I make the most of this exciting journey and share my insights with others, I have decided to document my areas of interest and progress publicly.

Our expedition begins

I have created a dynamic and diverse community called Engineers Group, consisting of professionals from various fields such as software engineering, project management, network engineering, and highly motivated students. Our primary objective is to learn and implement DevOps and Cloud practices. As part of this journey, I aim to document our progress over the next 120 days by covering all the foundational areas comprehensively. Moreover, I encourage all members of the community to contribute actively by sharing their experiences and resources to facilitate collaborative learning.

To kickstart this exciting journey, we will spend the first six days exploring the fundamentals of DevOps, followed by deeper dives into specific areas over the following 16 weeks. I also recommend that everyone creates a personal mind map to identify their interests and position themselves accordingly.

Additionally, I would like to share another valuable resource that I believe everyone should explore to enhance their understanding of DevOps. which is as follow:
DevOps Roadmap

Breaking down DevOps: Key concepts and principles

As we embark on the 120-day challenge of learning about DevOps, it's important to understand what it is and why it matters. While there are countless resources available online, I've distilled some high-level concepts that will be a great starting point for our journey.

Firstly, it's crucial to note that DevOps is not a tool that you can buy or download. It's not a programming language or some sort of mystical art either. Rather, it's a way of doing things more effectively in software development.

Despite its name, DevOps is not just for developers - anyone involved in software development, from operations to QA engineers, stands to benefit from learning about it. At its core, DevOps is a set of practices designed to speed up the time between ideation and product release. By adopting a DevOps mindset, teams can move from long, drawn-out release cycles to more frequent, smaller releases.

Another key aspect of DevOps is its close relationship with the Agile methodology. The two are often used together in order to achieve continuous delivery of applications.

One of the most important responsibilities of a DevOps engineer is to break down silos between different teams, including developers, operations, and QA. By bringing everyone together and encouraging collaboration, DevOps can help to streamline the entire development process.

Finally, automation plays a critical role in making DevOps effective and efficient. By automating tasks wherever possible, teams can reduce errors and speed up the overall process.

Resources

I am always open to adding additional resources.

My advice is to watch all of the below and hopefully you have also picked something up from the text and explanations above.

Thanks guys for exploring my knowledge of Day 1 hope to see you tomorrow for Day 2
item.author: codebyuwabor

item.title: 参加 dbt（数据构建工具）面试 | Acing the dbt (Data Build Tool) Interview
item.description:
姆温达·哈伦·姆巴布

发表于 6 月 12 日

在信息至高无上的数据分析世界中，企业依靠强大的工具来有效地管理和分析数据。

凭借其高效转换和分析数据的能力，dbt 已成为数据工程和分析领域的游戏规则改变者。

为了利用 dbt 的力量，组织需要熟练的专业人员来驾驭它的复杂性并释放它的能力。

因此，与 dbt 相关的工作面试对雇主和候选人都变得越来越重要。

如果您正在准备与 dbt 相关的工作面试或寻求评估候选人的 dbt 技能，请务必提出正确的问题。

为了帮助您解决这个问题，我们为每个级别编制了一份基本的 dbt 面试问题列表。 这些问题涵盖一系列主题，将评估候选人对 dbt 核心概念、功能和最佳实践的知识和理解。

初学者级。

问题 1.1：什么是 dbt，它与传统的 ETL/ELT 工具有何不同？

  • 与传统的 ETL/ELT 工具不同，dbt 专注于利用 SQL 和版本控制系统在数据仓库内转换数据。

回答：

它使用 SQL 和 YAML 配置文件来定义转换、模型和测试，从而可以轻松构建和维护数据管道。

问题 1.2：如何安装和设置 dbt？

  • 要安装和设置 dbt（数据构建工具），请按照下列步骤操作：

安装 Python：确保系统上安装了 Python。 安装 dbt：打开命令行界面 (CLI) 并运行以下命令以使用 pip（Python 包安装程序）安装 dbt：

设置 dbt 项目：为您的 dbt 项目创建一个新目录。 导航到 CLI 中的项目目录。

初始化项目：运行以下命令来初始化您的 dbt 项目：

配置你的项目：打开你项目目录下的dbt_project.yml文件，根据你的项目需要进行修改。 该文件包含项目级配置，例如目标数据库、连接信息和插件。

设置数据库连接：打开项目目录中的 profiles.yml 文件并配置数据库连接详细信息，包括数据库类型、主机、端口、用户名、密码和数据库名称。

测试设置：运行以下命令来测试您的 dbt 安装和项目设置：

如果一切设置正确，您应该会看到有关 dbt 项目和数据库连接的调试信息。

这样，您现在已经安装并设置了 dbt。 您可以开始使用 dbt 来构建、测试和部署您的数据模型。

问题 1.3：dbt 模型的目的是什么？

  • 它们可用于创建新表、视图或物化视图，并且它们充当数据分析的构建块。

问题 1.4：解释 dbt 中“来源”和“种子”的概念。

  • 来源是指用作 dbt 模型输入的外部数据表。 另一方面，种子是一种定义可在 dbt 项目中使用的静态或参考数据的方法。

中级水平。

问题 2.1：dbt 如何处理模式迁移？

  • 它跟踪对模型的更改并支持对数据仓库架构的增量更改。

问题 2.2：dbt 挂钩有哪些不同类型，你会在什么时候使用它们？

  • 它们可以是预挂钩（在构建模型之前）、后挂钩（在构建模型之后）或运行时挂钩（在运行特定 dbt 命令之前和之后）。 考生应解释每种挂钩类型的用例。

问题 2.2：您如何处理 dbt 中的增量或基于时间的数据加载？

  • 可以使用 dbt 的“合并”功能处理增量数据加载，该功能支持将源数据与目标表进行比较，以根据特定列执行插入、更新或更新插入。

问题 2.2：你能解释一下 dbt 宏是如何工作的吗？

  • 它们有助于简化复杂的逻辑、提高代码的可重用性并遵守最佳实践。

问题 3.1：如何优化 dbt 性能？

优化 dbt 性能对于高效的数据转换至关重要。 以下是提高 dbt 性能的一些策略：

  • 增量模型：利用增量模型仅处理和转换新的或更改的数据。 这减少了不必要的处理并提高了整体性能。

  • 缓存：配置dbt的缓存特性，用于存储之前执行模型的结果。 这有助于避免重复计算并加快后续运行。

  • 物化视图：利用物化视图预先计算和存储复杂或常用查询的结果。 物化视图提供对聚合或派生数据的更快访问。

  • 查询优化：分析和优化 dbt 模型中使用的 SQL 查询。 考虑为用于连接和过滤条件的列建立索引，优化子查询，并根据底层数据库使用适当的查询技术。

通过实施这些性能优化技术，您可以显着提高 dbt 转换的速度和效率。

问题 3.2：在 dbt 中进行测试的重要性是什么？您将如何为 dbt 模型编写测试？

  • 测试有助于验证数据完整性、业务规则合规性以及回归预防。 要为 dbt 模型编写测试，您可以使用 dbt 提供的内置测试框架，利用测试宏根据列存在、数据类型、关系或值等特定要求定义测试。

问题 3.3：您能描述一下将 dbt 与版本控制系统集成的过程吗？

将 dbt 与版本控制系统 (VCS) 集成可以实现有效协作、代码管理和跟踪 dbt 项目中的更改。

下面是将 dbt 与 VCS 集成的分步过程：

  • 设置版本控制存储库：选择一个 VCS 平台（例如 Git、GitHub、GitLab）并创建一个新的存储库来存储您的 dbt 项目的代码。

  • 将 dbt 初始化为 Git 存储库：在命令行界面或终端中导航到 dbt 项目的根目录。

运行以下命令：

 git init git add . git commit -m "Initial commit"
  • 运行以下命令，替换为您的远程存储库的 URL：

     git remote add origin <remote-repo-url>
    • 将本地存储库推送到远程存储库：使用以下命令将本地 dbt 项目代码上传到远程存储库：
     git push -u origin master

    • 协作和管理更改：集成完成后，您现在可以与您的团队就 dbt 项目进行协作。 每个团队成员都可以克隆存储库，在他们的本地环境中进行更改，并使用 Git 命令（git add、git commit、git push）将他们的更改推送到远程存储库。

    • 分支和拉取请求：利用 Git 分支策略来处理单独的功能或实验。 当准备好合并更改时，团队成员可以在 VCS 平台上创建拉取请求，允许代码审查并将更改无缝集成到主分支中。

    通过将 dbt 与版本控制系统集成，您可以建立一个结构化的协作开发环境，从而实现有效的团队合作、变更跟踪以及必要时回滚变更的能力。

    问题 3.4：您使用过 dbt 包吗？ 解释它们的用途以及如何使用它们。

    • 考生应讨论如何安装、使用和创建 dbt 包。

    更多练习题。

    使用 dbt 有什么好处？ 如何编写 dbt 模型？ 你如何运行dbt？ 您如何使用 dbt 来处理数据质量问题？ 您如何使用 dbt 来管理数据沿袭？ 您如何使用 dbt 将更改部署到生产中？ 您如何使用 dbt 来测试您的数据管道？ 您如何使用 dbt 与其他数据工程师协作？ 如何使用 dbt 创建自定义宏？ 您如何使用 dbt 与其他数据工具集成？ 您如何使用 dbt 来自动化您的数据工作流？ 您如何使用 dbt 来扩展您的数据工程工作？

    您如何使用 dbt 来创建数据驱动的文化？

这些只是基本的 dbt 面试问题的几个例子。 您将被问到的具体问题将取决于您面试的职位和面试官的经验水平。 但是，这些问题应该为您准备面试提供一个良好的起点。

除了这些技术问题之外，您可能还会被问及有关您使用 dbt 的体验的行为问题。

这些问题将评估您在协作、沟通和解决问题等领域的技能和能力。 一定要练习回答这些类型的问题。

如果您刚开始使用 dbt，以下是一些您可能会觉得有用的资源：

转型快乐，万事如意。

一旦解除暂停，grayhat 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，grayhat 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 grayhat 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。
